title: All-Embracing Multiphysics Analysis for Chiplet-Based Systems - Semiwiki
gist: Synopsys, following its Ansys acquisition, has released Multiphysics Fusion solutions for chiplet-based multi-die designs, integrating electrical, thermal, electromagnetic, and mechanical analysis in a unified platform. Customers MediaTek, NVIDIA, and Samsung report up to 3x faster IR-drop-aware STA signoff and 10x faster multiphysics design closure. As AI datacenter systems combine CPUs, GPUs, and HBM stacks in single packages, full multiphysics signoff has moved from optional to non-negotiable.

insights.claim: Synopsys, following its Ansys acquisition, released its first Multiphysics Fusion solutions for chiplets and multi-die designs, integrating RedHawk-SC, RedHawk-SC Electrothermal, and HFSS-IC within a unified 3DIC Compiler environment.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: Validates the strategic rationale for the Synopsys-Ansys deal; creates a defensible, all-in-one platform that rival EDA vendors cannot easily replicate.
insights.quote: Following the acquisition of Ansys, Synopsys has released its first Multiphysics Fusion solutions for chiplets and for Multi-Die Designs.

insights.claim: MediaTek, NVIDIA, and Samsung validated Synopsys' Multiphysics Fusion platform, reporting up to 3x faster IR-drop-aware STA signoff and 10x faster multiphysics design closure across individual die and multi-die designs.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: Third-party customer validation from top-tier chip companies de-risks the tool's adoption story and signals a revenue ramp for Synopsys' EDA+silicon analysis suite.
insights.quote: up to 3X faster IR-drop-aware STA signoff, while accelerating multiphysics design closure by 10X within individual die and across multi-die designs

themes.theme: Multi-Die Complexity Driving New Reliability Requirements

insights.claim: AI datacenter systems combining CPUs, GPUs, and HBM stacks in a single package generate extreme thermal loads from GPU power consumption, risking die warpage, delamination, broken contacts, and electromigration-driven failures.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: Thermal and mechanical failure modes in multi-die AI packages are elevating demand for advanced signoff tools, creating a durable TAM for Synopsys and competing EDA vendors.
insights.quote: Thermal load generated by this power draw can throttle performance and trigger mechanical failures, die warpage leading to delamination, broken contacts and more.

insights.claim: Automotive electronics and wireless infrastructure (cell towers) require multi-die systems to meet 15-20 year lifetimes, significantly exceeding consumer electronics expectations and demanding tighter multiphysics control.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: Long-lifecycle industrial and automotive end-markets represent a growing, non-cyclical demand driver for multiphysics EDA tools beyond AI datacenters.
insights.quote: Modern automotive electronics is required to meet safety standards over 15–20 year lifetimes, significantly longer than expectations for consumer electronics.

insights.claim: Electric vehicle power switching devices operate near 200°C junction temperatures, introducing parasitic oscillations and electromagnetic interference that threaten reliability in both drivetrain and cabin electronics.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: EV electronics complexity is a growing vertical for multiphysics EDA tools, diversifying Synopsys' revenue beyond AI and mobile.
insights.quote: Power switching devices operate at high voltages and high frequencies, with junction temperatures approaching 200oC creating similar thermal challenges.

insights.claim: Standalone analysis of individual physical domains—thermal, mechanical, electromagnetic, signal integrity—is no longer sufficient for chiplet-based designs; only concurrent full multiphysics analysis can ensure reliability across intended use-cases.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: This structural shift in design methodology drives tool refresh cycles and creates upsell opportunities from single-domain tools to integrated multiphysics platforms.
insights.quote: The only way to accurately model behavior to ensure device reliability in the intended implementation and across a wide range of use-cases is through full multiphysics analysis, concurrent with design.

insights.claim: The Synopsys-Ansys merger delivers its first concrete product output in unified EMIR, thermal, signal integrity, and electromechanical stress signoff within a single 3DIC Compiler environment, confirming the strategic thesis of the deal.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: Investors who tracked the M&A rationale see early proof that the combined entity can deliver differentiated, multi-domain products that neither company could build as fast alone.
insights.quote: Looks like exactly what we expected from a Synopsys/Ansys merger, advancing systems performance and reliability to the demands of leading-edge multi-die designs.

insights.claim: Synopsys' Multiphysics Fusion also extends to analog and photonics components in multi-die systems, broadening the addressable market beyond digital chiplet designs.
insights.speaker: Bernard Murphy
insights.so_what: Analog and photonics multiphysics coverage increases Synopsys' TAM as co-packaged optics and silicon photonics gain adoption in AI infrastructure.
insights.quote: Similar improvements apply for analog and photonics components in multi-die systems.
